footnotes.a: Related is defined as reasonable possibility that the AE was caused by investigational product, as assessed by investigator. The causality will be judged "related" when the causality is missing.

deliverables.title: Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term - <<After Cell Infusion>> (Safety Set)
programming_notes: Subjects with multiple occurrences are counted once per system organ class and preferred term regardless of the number of occurrences. The table is sorted by international order for system organ class and by decreasing number of subjects for preferred term based on the total number of subjects. They will be sorted alphabetically when they have the same total frequency within the same level.

deliverables.title: Adverse Events (Safety Set)

footnotes.b: Study Day = (date of event – CAR-T cell infusion date +1) if the event date is on or later than reference start date, Study Day = (date of event – CAR-T cell infusion date) if the event date is earlier than CAR-T cell infusion date. Study day at end is missing if it is ongoing at the data cut-off.
footnotes.c: Duration (days) = End date or Date of  when the AE is ongoing – Start date +1

programming_notes: Multiple records will be listed if the AE has undergone an AE change. An AE change includes the change of the CTCAE grade, the causality, and the action taken.
table_data: Study day at start/Study day at end [b]
AE possibly related to CART therapy [d]

deliverables.title: Cytokine Release Syndrome (Safety Set)

footnotes.a: Study Day = (date of event – CAR-T cell infusion date +1) if the event date is on or later than reference start date, Study Day = (date of event – CAR-T cell infusion date) if the event date is earlier than CAR-T cell infusion date. Study day at end is missing if it is ongoing at the data cut-off.
footnotes.b: Duration (days) = End date or Date of  when the AE is ongoing – Start date +1
footnotes.c: The severity of cytokine release syndromes are analysed according to American Society of Transplantation and Cellular Therapy (ASTCT) grading criteria in 2019, grade 1-5.

footnotes.c: The severity of immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndromes are analysed according to American Society of Transplantation and Cellular Therapy (ASTCT) grading criteria in 2019, grade 1-5.

Subjects with multiple occurrences are counted once per system organ class and preferred term regardless of the number of occurrences. The table is sorted by international order for system organ class and by decreasing number of subjects for preferred term based on the total number of subjects. They will be sorted alphabetically when they have the same total frequency within the same level.
Multiple records will be listed if the AE has undergone an AE change. An AE change includes the change of the CTCAE grade, the causality, and the action taken.
